prompt: Implement a PropertyOps Maintenance & Compliance Backend for offline-first multifamily property operations that manages tenant maintenance, risk controls, notifications, payments, and operational analytics within a single-node Docker deployment. The system must operate fully offline on a local network without any dependency on internet connectivity or external third-party services. Core roles include Tenant (creates and rates requests), Technician (accepts and updates assignments), Property Manager (dispatch, cost approval, SLA oversight), Compliance Reviewer (moderates reports and enforces actions), and System Administrator (configuration and audit). The backend uses Gin to expose resource-oriented API capability groups for Authentication, Tenants, Work Orders, Notifications, Payments, Governance, and Analytics, with request/response structures defined by typed fields and validation rules. Maintenance requests support description (required, 20-2,000 chars), priority enum (Low/Normal/High/Emergency), up to 6 locally stored images (JPEG/PNG, each <= 5 MB, validated by MIME and file signature), and preferred access window (MM/DD/YYYY + 12-hour time). On submission, the system assigns an SLA target (Emergency: 4 hours, High: 24 hours, Normal: 72 hours, Low: 5 business days) and auto-dispatches to an on-site technician pool using a deterministic round-robin by skill tag; managers may reassign with a required reason (10-500 chars). Work orders track status transitions (New->Assigned->In Progress->Awaiting Approval->Completed->Archived), time stamps, labor/material cost lines in USD with two decimals, responsibility attribution (Tenant/Vendor/Property), and post-completion rating (1-5) plus optional feedback (<= 1,000 chars). A governance capability allows users and staff to file reports against tenants, work orders, and message threads with category enums and evidence attachments; items enter a manual review queue with enforcement actions including warning, rate limit (e.g., max 10 submissions/hour), and suspension (1 day, 7 days, or indefinite). Notifications are limited to in-app message records only (no SMS/email/official-account delivery in offline mode), supporting templates, scheduled sends, event-triggered sends (e.g., SLA breach), read receipts, and retry rules for internal delivery jobs. Data persistence uses MySQL via GORM with tables for users, roles, sessions, tenant profiles, properties/units, work_orders, work_order_events (append-only audit), cost_items, attachments (hash, size, mime, path), notifications, notification_receipts, reports, keywords/blacklist, risk_rules, payments, and reconciliation_runs; key indexes include unique username, work_order by property+status+created_at, and report by target_type+target_id. Passwords must be salted and hashed (Argon2id or bcrypt with cost >= 12), sessions expire after 30 minutes idle with a maximum of 7 days, and sensitive fields (access codes, limited PII such as phone numbers if stored) are encrypted at rest using AES-256 with key rotation every 180 days; responses mask PII by default and require elevated roles plus explicit purpose to export. Payment capabilities are adapted for offline operation: the system records payment intents and settlement postings without any external payment-provider callbacks; intents expire after 30 minutes if not marked paid by an authorized manager, reconciliation runs produce daily statements from locally recorded postings, and discrepancy workflows support make-up posting or reversal with mandatory dual-approval above $500.00. Analytics and operations dashboards are computed from local event data, providing popularity (most-requested issue types), conversion funnel (request->assigned->completed), retention (repeat requests per unit over 30/90 days), search-term analysis over work order tags, and quality metrics such as negative rating rate and report rate; custom reports allow saved filters and CSV export, with scheduled report generation stored locally (no outbound email). Non-functional requirements include p95 API latency under 300 ms for typical queries at 50 RPS on a single host, full auditability of state changes, configurable retention (default 7 years for financial records, 2 years for message content), daily encrypted backups with restore validation, built-in health checks and log query APIs, and reserved interfaces for anomaly monitoring/alert ingestion limited to local integrations without credentials, keys, or internet dependencies.
